pattern: A value g is achievable as some subsequence's gcd exactly when the gcd of all array elements divisible by g equals g, so test every candidate divisor by folding the gcd of its multiples present.
hints: The gcd of a subsequence can only be one of the divisors of the values present.
For a fixed candidate g, the best chance to realize it is to take every element that is a multiple of g.
For each g up to the maximum value, walk multiples g, 2g, 3g... that appear in the array, fold their gcd, and count g when that running gcd equals g.

edge_inputs.description: small set with five distinct gcds
value one forces gcd one only via itself

stress.description: small array so the exponential subsequence oracle stays fast
